[S1] 这一壮举几乎让奥娜格自己也乱了方寸；但女人的机智和镇定有什么做不到的呢？
[S2] " 你现在进来吧， " 她说， " 吃点我们能提供的简陋食物。 "
[S3] " 芬，即使你和他是敌人，也不会不屑于在他自己家里善待你；而且，说真的，如果我在他不在时没这么做，他也会不高兴。 "
[S4] 于是她带他进来，在他面前放了半打我们之前提到的面包，还有一两罐黄油、一块煮咸肉和一堆卷心菜，让他自便——要知道，这是在土豆发明很久之前。
[S5] 法鲁阿——顺便说一句，他既是英雄也是贪吃鬼——把一块面包塞进嘴里，正要咬一大口时，芬和奥娜格都被一声介于咆哮和尖叫之间的声音惊呆了。
[S6] " 血和愤怒！ " 他大喊。
[S7] " 这是怎么回事？ "
[S8] " 我掉了两颗牙！ "
[S9] " 你给我的是什么面包？ "
[S10] " 怎么了？ " 奥娜格冷静地问。
[S11] " 怎么了！ " 对方吼道。
[S12] " 哎呀，我嘴里最好的两颗牙没了。 "
[S13] " 哎呀， " 她说， " 那是芬的面包——他在家时唯一吃的面包；但说真的，我忘了告诉你，除了他自己和摇篮里的那个孩子，没人能吃这个。 "
[S14] " 不过，我想既然你被传说是你这种体型中相当强壮的小个子，也许你能应付，而且我不想冒犯一个自以为能打败芬的人。 "
[S15] " 这里还有一块——也许没那块硬。 "
[S16] 法鲁阿此刻不仅饿，而且饿极了，于是他再次咬向第二块面包，立刻听到一声比第一声还响两倍的惨叫。
[S17] " 雷公和内脏！ " 他吼道， " 把你的面包拿走，否则我一颗牙也保不住了；又掉了两颗。 "
[S18] " 好吧，老实人， " 奥娜格回答， " 如果你吃不了这面包就安静地说，别吵醒摇篮里的孩子。 "
[S19] " 看，现在他被我吵醒了！ "
[S20] 芬现在发出一声尖叫，吓坏了巨人，因为这声音来自一个被描述为如此年幼的孩子。
[S21] " 妈妈， " 他说， " 我饿了——给我点吃的。 "
[S22] 奥娜格走过去，递给他一块没有夹石头的面包——芬的胃口被眼前的一切刺激得更旺盛了，很快就把它吃光了。
[S23] 法鲁阿惊呆了，暗自庆幸自己运气好没遇到芬，因为，他对自己说，我对一个能吃这种面包的人毫无胜算，连他摇篮里的儿子都能在我眼前大嚼特嚼。
[S24] " 我想看一眼摇篮里的孩子， " 他对奥娜格说， " 因为我告诉你，能消化这种营养的婴儿可不是闹着玩的，在食物短缺的夏天更是如此。 "
selector_reason: S13 explains the bread's property, S15 introduces the second cake, S16 and S17 directly describe the outcome of biting into it (teeth loss).

Full context:
[S0] she then brought far rua down to see the place , which was then all one solid rock ; and after looking at it for some time , he cracked his right middle finger nine times , and , stooping down , tore a cleft about four hundred feet deep and a quarter of a mile in length , which has since been christened by the name of lumford 's glen .
[S1] this feat nearly threw oonagh herself off her guard ; but what wo n't a woman 's sagacity and presence of mind accomplish ?
[S2] " you 'll now come in , " said she , " and eat a bit of such humble fare as we can give . "
[S3] " finn , even though you and he were enemies , would scorn not to treat you kindly in his own house ; and , indeed , if i did n't do it even in his absence , he would not be pleased with me . "
[S4] she accordingly brought him in , and placing half a dozen of the cakes we spoke of before him , together with a can or two of butter , a side of boiled bacon , and a stack of cabbage , she desired him to help himself -- for this , be it known , was long before the invention of potatoes .
[S5] far rua , who , by the way , was a glutton as well as a hero , put one of the cakes in his mouth to take a huge whack out of it , when both finn and oonagh were stunned with a noise that resembled something between a growl and a yell .
[S6] " blood and fury ! " he shouted out .
[S7] " how is this ? "
[S8] " here are two of my teeth out ! "
[S9] " what kind of bread is this you gave me ? "
[S10] " what 's the matter ? " said oonagh coolly .
[S11] " matter ! " shouted the other .
[S12] " why , here are two of the best teeth in my head gone . "
[S13] " why , " said she , " that 's finn 's bread -- the only bread he ever eats when at home ; but , indeed , i forgot to tell you that nobody can eat it but himself and that child in the cradle there . "
[S14] " i thought , however , that as you were reported to be rather a stout little fellow of your size you might be able to manage it , and i did not wish to affront a man that thinks himself able to fight finn . "
[S15] " here 's another cake -- maybe it 's not so hard as that . "
[S16] far rua , at the moment , was not only hungry , but ravenous , so he accordingly made a fresh set at the second cake , and immediately another yell was heard twice as loud as the first .
[S17] " thunder and giblets ! " he roared , " take your bread out of this , or i will not have a tooth in my head ; there 's another pair of them gone . "
[S18] " well , honest man , " replied oonagh , " if you 're not able to eat the bread say so quietly , and do n't be awakening the child in the cradle there . "
[S19] " there , now , he 's awake upon me ! "
[S20] finn now gave a skirl that frightened the giant , as coming from such a youngster as he was represented to be .
[S21] " mother , " said he , " i 'm hungry -- get me something to eat . "
[S22] oonagh went over , and putting into his hand a cake that had no griddle in it -- finn , whose appetite in the meantime was sharpened by what he saw going forward , soon made it disappear .
[S23] far rua was thunderstruck , and secretly thanked his stars that he had the good fortune to miss meeting finn , for , as he said to himself , i 'd have no chance with a man who could eat such bread as that , which even his son that 's in the cradle can munch before my eyes .
[S24] " i 'd like to take a glimpse at the lad in the cradle , " said he to oonagh , " for i can tell you that the infant who can manage that nutriment is no joke to look at or to feed of a scarce summer . "
[S25] " with all the veins of my heart , " replied oonagh .
[S26] " get up , acushla , and show this decent little man something that wo n't be unworthy of your father , finn m'coul . "
[S27] finn , who was dressed for the occasion as much like a boy as possible , got up , and bringing far rua out , " are you strong ? " said he .
[S28] " thunder and ounze ! " exclaimed the other , " what a voice in so small a chap ! "
[S29] " are you strong ? " said finn again .
[S30] " are you able to squeeze water out of that white stone ? " he asked , putting one into far rua 's hand .
[S31] the latter squeezed and squeezed the stone , but to no purpose ; he might pull the rocks of lumford 's glen asunder , and flatten a thunderbolt , but to squeeze water out of a white stone was beyond his strength .
[S32] finn eyed him with great contempt as he kept straining and squeezing and squeezing and straining till he got black in the face with the efforts .
[S33] " ah , you 're a poor creature , " said finn .
[S34] " you a giant ! "
[S35] " give me the stone here , and when i 'll show what finn 's little son can do you may then judge of what my daddy himself is . "
[S36] finn then took the stone , and then , slyly exchanging it for the curds , he squeezed the latter until the whey , as clear as water , oozed out in a little shower from his hand .
[S37] " i 'll now go in , " said he , " to my cradle ; for i scorn to lose my time with anyone that 's not able to eat my daddy 's bread , or squeeze water out of a stone . "
[S38] " bedad , you had better be off out of this before he comes back , for if he catches you , it 's in flummery he 'd have you in two minutes . "
[S39] far rua , seeing what he had seen , was of the same opinion himself ; his knees knocked together with the terror of finn 's return , and he accordingly hastened in to bid oonagh farewell , and to assure her that , from that day out , he never wished to hear of , much less to see , her husband .
[S40] " i admit fairly that i 'm not a match for him , " said he , " strong as i am . "
[S41] " tell him i will avoid him as i would the plague , and that i will make myself scarce in this part of the country while i live . "
[S42] finn , in the meantime , had gone into the cradle , where he lay very quietly , his heart in his mouth with delight that far rua was about to take his departure without discovering the tricks that been played off on him .
[S43] " it 's well for you , " said oonagh , " that he does n't happen to be here , for it 's nothing but hawk 's meat he 'd make of you . "
[S44] " i know that , " said far rua , " divel a thing else he 'd make of me ; but , before i go , will you let me feel what kind of teeth they are that can eat griddle - cakes like that ? "
[S45] and he pointed to it as he spoke .
[S46] " with all the pleasure in life , " says she ; " only as they 're far back in his head you must put your finger a good way in . "
[S47] far rua was surprised to find so powerful a set of grinders in one so young ; but he was still much more so on finding , when he took his hand from finn 's mouth , that he had left the very finger upon which his whole strength depended behind him .
[S48] he gave one loud groan and fell down at once with terror and weakness .
[S49] this was all finn wanted , who now knew that his most powerful and bitterest enemy was completely at his mercy .
[S50] he instantly started out of the cradle , and in a few minutes the great far rua , that was for such a length of time the terror of him and all his followers , was no more .
